summary: Matched the small fighter source file `melee/ft/ft_07C1.c` at 100%, replacing the old assembly object and deleting `asm/melee/ft/ft_07C1.s`. The match covered five functions (`ft_8007C114`, `ft_8007C17C`, `ft_8007C224`, `ft_8007C2E0`, `ft_8007C4BC`) totaling 1.31 KB. The main source adjustment was in `ft_8007C4BC`, where a helper inline was removed and its logic was folded back into the function with scoped locals and explicit stack padding to satisfy codegen.

key_files.role: Matched C implementation; `ft_8007C4BC` was restructured for exact codegen.
Deleted now that the C file matches.

what_changed: Updated `obj_files.mk` so the build links `src/melee/ft/ft_07C1.c.o` instead of the assembly object.
The PR report shows `melee/ft/ft_07C1.c` at 100.00% with score 0 for the file and for `ft_8007C114`, `ft_8007C17C`, `ft_8007C224`, `ft_8007C2E0`, and `ft_8007C4BC`.
Removed `static inline Fighter_GObj* inlineB0(Fighter* ft)` from `src/melee/ft/ft_07C1.c` and placed its grab-attacker selection logic directly inside `ft_8007C4BC`.
Introduced `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)` in `ft_8007C4BC` near the local `grab_attacker` declaration.
Rescoped locals in `ft_8007C4BC`: `fp1` and `throw_hitbox` are declared in an inner block, and `HitCapsule* hit` is declared inside the collision-processing block.

Kept the fighter iteration over `HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters`, the `seen` gate, `grab_attacker` exclusion, and calls to `ftColl_8007B868`, `lbColl_80007AFC`, `ft_8007C2E0`, and `lbColl_800089B8` semantically intact.
smart_moves: Inlining the former `inlineB0` body manually into `ft_8007C4BC` gave finer control over local ordering, casts, and stack layout than relying on a separate static inline helper.
The explicit `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)` is a targeted matching tactic: it acknowledges an otherwise-unused stack-layout requirement without obscuring the actual control flow.

Nested scopes were used to influence lifetime/register allocation of `fp1`, `throw_hitbox`, and `hit`, which is often necessary for matching Metrowerks output in small functions with many early `continue` paths.
The PR preserved readable high-level control flow rather than falling back to asm or excessive gotos: the loop remains a normal `for` over fighters with structured conditionals.
Build metadata and object lists were updated in the same PR as the asm deletion, avoiding a stale asm/C mismatch.
decomp_lessons: For matching, a helper marked `static inline` can still produce different stack or register allocation than hand-inlining the same logic in the caller. If a nearly matched function is sensitive to local lifetimes, try folding the helper body back into the parent function.
Declaration placement matters: moving locals into narrower scopes can change generated code enough to eliminate the final mismatch.
Use `PAD_STACK` sparingly but explicitly when the source is semantically correct and the remaining mismatch is stack-shape related.
When replacing an asm file, update all build selectors together: the project config (`configure.py`) and object list (`obj_files.mk`) both need to point at the C object.

Small file matches are still useful knowledge records because they often capture reusable codegen tricks, here specifically manual inlining, scope shaping, and stack padding in fighter collision code.
naming_conventions: Matched functions retained address-style names: `ft_8007C114`, `ft_8007C17C`, `ft_8007C224`, `ft_8007C2E0`, and `ft_8007C4BC`.
The file name follows the fighter module address-range convention: `ft_07C1.c` / `ft_07C1.s`.
Scratch entries use both older `func_8007C4BC` and newer module-prefixed `ft_8007C4BC` naming; this PR added `ft_8007C4BC` OK records.

Existing struct/field names in the edited code include `victim_gobj`, `dmg.x1868_source`, `x1064_thrownHitbox`, `x221C_b6`, `x2219_b1`, `x2227_b2`, and `x221F_b4`; no new field naming convention was introduced by the PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the matching asm file only after the C report showed 100.00% for the file.
Changed the object file from `$(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/ft/ft_07C1.s.o` to `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/ft_07C1.c.o`.
Removed the `inlineB0` helper and directly implemented the `grab_attacker` selection in `ft_8007C4BC`.
Used explicit casts from `fp->victim_gobj` and `fp->dmg.x1868_source` to `Fighter_GObj*` when assigning to `grab_attacker`.
Used nested blocks to shape local lifetimes around `Fighter* fp1`, `HitCapsule* throw_hitbox`, and `HitCapsule* hit`.

review_feedback.text: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR dump slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that use `PAD_STACK` to complete a fighter-function match.
Find later renames or documentation for `ft_8007C4BC` and `x1064_thrownHitbox`.
Search for other matches where a `static inline` helper was removed or manually inlined for codegen.
Look up neighboring fighter files `ft_07C6.c` and `ftcoll.c` for related collision or throw-hitbox decomp patterns.
